literature-report-plan.name: R1-post-training-methods
literature-report-plan.invocation-prompt:
create literature report on "Post-Training Methodologies for Medical Segmentation"
Focus: SWA variants, model soup, temperature scaling, Platt scaling, isotonic regression, conformal calibration on SMALL datasets (70 volumes) with 3D medical segmentation. How post-training transforms interact with loss function choice. What the factorial design should measure.
Context: This report informs the NEUROVEX manuscript's post-training pipeline section and the factorial experiment design. The platform already has SWA, temperature scaling, and conformal prediction stubs. We need to know what ELSE to implement and how to evaluate it.
EXCLUDED topics (covered in other reports): - Agentic AI (report 5) - Ensemble methods beyond model averaging (report R2) - Federated learning (report R3) - Regulatory compliance (report R4)
Repo context: /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops KG domains to update: training, models Manuscript section: Methods: Post-Training Pipeline (R2b)

seed-papers.paper: Izmailov et al. (2018). "Averaging Weights Leads to Wider Optima and Better Generalization." UAI. https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.05407
Wortsman et al. (2022). "Model Soups: Averaging Weights of Multiple Fine-tuned Models Improves Accuracy without Increasing Inference Time." ICML. https://arxiv.org/abs/2203.05482
Guo et al. (2017). "On Calibration of Modern Neural Networks." ICML. https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.04599
Mehrtash et al. (2020). "Confidence Calibration and Predictive Uncertainty Estimation for Deep Medical Image Segmentation." IEEE TMI. https://doi.org/10.1109/TMI.2020.3006437
Angelopoulos et al. (2022). "Conformal Risk Control." ICLR 2024. https://arxiv.org/abs/2208.02814
Kofler et al. (2023). "Are we using appropriate segmentation metrics?" arXiv. https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.07396
Mukhoti et al. (2023). "Deep Deterministic Uncertainty." ICML. https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.11582
Cha et al. (2021). "SWAD: Domain Generalization by Seeking Flat Minima." NeurIPS. https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.08604
Maddox et al. (2019). "A Simple Baseline for Bayesian Inference in Deep Learning." NeurIPS. https://arxiv.org/abs/1902.02476
Nishi et al. (2024). "Temperature Scaling for Medical Image Segmentation." arXiv. https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.12024

search-domains.domain: stochastic weight averaging medical imaging small dataset
model soup segmentation fine-tuning weight averaging
